name: Education Personnel
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving employees of Vermilion County Schools, offering competitive loan rates, deposit accounts, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Education Personnel Federal Credit Union (EPFCU) was established to serve the unique financial needs of employees within Vermilion County Schools. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates on the not-for-profit, member-owned model standard to the credit union industry, meaning earnings are returned to members rather than shareholders. The institution is NCUA-insured and emphasizes the credit union difference—prioritizing people over profits.

EPFCU offers a comprehensive suite of personal financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, holiday club accounts, youth accounts, vehicle loans, personal loans, real estate loans, credit cards, and home equity loans. Members have access to online banking with enhanced features including account transfers, lost card shutdown capabilities, and bill pay functionality. The credit union also provides Visa debit cards, ATM network access, a mobile app, investment services, insurance products, and loan calculators. Notably, they advertise student loan services, though specific details are not elaborated on the website.

The credit union distinguishes itself through membership-exclusive eligibility (limited to Vermilion County Schools employees), competitive loan rates ranging from 3.89% APR for vehicle loans to 6.89% APR for signature loans, and community-focused values. They highlight their participation in school-based banking branches to promote financial literacy among young people and their commitment to serving underbanked populations as an alternative to predatory lenders. Enhanced online banking infrastructure with modern features reflects a commitment to digital convenience.

While EPFCU provides solid foundational banking services with competitive rates, the website lacks detailed disclosures on fee schedules, deposit rates, specific eligibility requirements beyond employer affiliation, and comprehensive loan terms. The organization's limited scope to a single county employer base may restrict growth and service expansion compared to larger regional or national credit unions. Potential members should verify current rates and complete fee information directly, as the website references but does not display detailed rate and fee schedules.
services: Savings accounts and special savings accounts
Online banking with bill pay and fund transfers
Visa debit cards and travel/gift cards
Loan calculators and car buying tools
pros: Competitive vehicle loan rates starting as low as 3.89% APR
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members rather than external shareholders
Enhanced online banking platform with multi-account access, transfers to other institutions, and lost card shutdown features
Mobile app and online bill pay for digital convenience
Signature loan rates as low as 6.89% APR for personal borrowing
Mortgage rates starting as low as 4.89% APR for real estate financing
Community commitment including school-based banking branches for financial education
cons: Membership restricted to Vermilion County Schools employees, limiting accessibility to general population
Website does not display actual fee schedules or deposit rates—users must call or visit in-person for complete pricing
Limited geographic service area (single county), reducing convenience for members outside the region
Student loan services mentioned but not detailed or explained on website
No apparent mention of investment advisory services or wealth management offerings beyond basic investments and insurance
best_for: Vermilion County Schools employees seeking member-owned banking with competitive rates
Individuals prioritizing credit union values and community-focused financial institutions over for-profit banks
School district workers looking for employer-affiliated financial services and educational banking partnerships
diagnosis: Education Personnel FCU is best for Vermilion County Schools employees seeking member-owned banking with competitive loan rates and digital convenience. The main caveat is that membership is restricted by employer affiliation, and detailed pricing information (fee schedules and deposit rates) must be obtained directly from the institution rather than online.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Education Personnel — Danville, IL Credit Union | CreditDoc
